matches.x: she came to prove Solomon with hard questions at. Jerusalem, with a very great company, and camels. that bare, spices, and gold in abundance, and precious stones; and when she was come to Solomon, she communed with him of all that was in her heart.

ships of Tarshish/luxury ships. Tarshish, whose precise location is unknown, was probably a prosperous and bustling Mediterranean seaport. For instance, it was through Tarshish that Solomon imported such luxury items as gold, silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks (1 Kgs. 10:22). Perhaps because of the city's connection with wealth and affluence, the destruction of Tarshish and its ships symbolizes…

4. The king had also other rulers, who were over the land of Syria and
of the Philistines, which reached from the river Euphrates to Egypt, and
these collected his tributes of the nations. Now these contributed to
the king's table, and to his supper every day 3 thirty cori of fine
flour, and sixty of meal; as also ten fat oxen, and twenty oxen out of
the pastures, and a hundred fat lambs; all…
